skill.name: minimax-music-playlist
skill.description: Generate personalized AI music playlists by analyzing music taste, planning songs, creating tracks with mmx, generating cover art, and saving feedback for future playlists.
skill.summary: Create personalized AI playlists with music generation, cover art, playback, and taste feedback.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. The weak-crypto and many backtick detections are false positives from Markdown examples, but the skill does intentionally direct agents to run local commands, read music history, call external services, and cache profile data. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, so publication is reasonable with privacy and command-execution warnings.

medium_findings.title: Local Music History Collection And Persistent Profiling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to collect Apple Music, Spotify export, and manual listening data, extract music taste signals, and store profile and feedback data. It excludes ip_addr and says not to show raw track lists, but music history is still sensitive personal data that needs explicit consent and storage disclosure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly describe data sources, extracted fields, filtering, profile caching, and feedback updates. The intent appears functional rather than malicious, so the issue is privacy risk rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Executed Local Commands And Media Tools
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of npm, mmx, osascript, find, open, media players, and directory listing. These commands support the playlist workflow, but they create real command execution and local environment interaction risk when run by an agent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and tool names are explicit, and several would execute locally or launch applications. They are scoped to music generation and playback, so this is elevated operational risk rather than evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: External Service Calls With User Taste Context
medium_findings.description: The skill points users to Spotify, queries MusicBrainz for artist metadata, and uses mmx services to generate music and cover images. Artist names, playlist themes, and generation prompts may leave the local machine, so users should understand what data is sent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Spotify and MusicBrainz network destinations are directly cited, and mmx generation commands imply use of an external generation service. No evidence shows covert transmission of secrets or sensitive fields.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak-cryptography matches do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited areas contain metadata, prose, music terminology, file extensions, or command flags, with no hash or encryption operation found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing, or encryption behavior at these locations. The alerts are consistent with substring matches in Markdown and music-generation text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Mostly Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance alerts at cache and example-text lines are false positives. The actual broad home-directory search is already covered as local command and filesystem risk because it looks for Spotify export files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Only line 65 performs a discovery-style command, and it is limited by max depth and filename patterns. The other cited lines describe cache files or example prose, not host reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Search Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the agent to search under the user home directory for Spotify export files. This is functional, but it should be run only with user consent because it inspects local filesystem paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and targets the user home directory. It is limited to expected Spotify filenames, which reduces but does not remove privacy risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Background Generation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows parallel mmx music and image generation commands that write media files to the user music folder. Agents should avoid unapproved spending, quota use, or file creation when invoking external generation tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly shown and include output paths and concurrent execution. The risk depends on the mmx CLI behavior and user authorization, so confidence is high for the pattern but not for malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Artist Metadata Lookup
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to query MusicBrainz for unknown artists and cache the results. This can reveal artist names from a user listening profile to a third-party service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destination and data purpose are explicit in the text. The line also includes a rate limit and cache instruction, which supports legitimate use but confirms network disclosure.

content.value_statement: Personalized playlist creation is slow when music history, mood, and style preferences are scattered across apps. This skill builds a taste profile, generates matching songs and cover art, then saves feedback for better future playlists.
actual_capabilities: Scans Apple Music, Spotify exports, or manual input to build a music taste profile.
Summarizes genre, mood, vocal, tempo, language, and top artist preferences.
Plans a themed playlist with genre mix, mood, vocal style, and song descriptions.
Uses mmx commands to generate songs and square album cover art.
Plays generated audio files with available local players when supported.
Saves playlist metadata and feedback to improve later recommendations.
limitations: Requires the mmx CLI, authentication, Python 3, and an audio player for the full workflow.
Spotify history requires a user-provided export because useful local data is not available.
MusicBrainz enrichment may miss artists or return incomplete genre metadata.
The skill stores taste profiles and feedback locally unless the user removes those files.

use_cases.title: Generate A Mood-Based Playlist
use_cases.description: Create a short custom playlist for a scene such as commuting, studying, or late-night listening.

use_cases.title: Prototype AI Music Concepts
use_cases.description: Turn audience taste signals into draft songs and cover art for creative review.

use_cases.title: Explore Listening Patterns
use_cases.description: Summarize personal genre, mood, vocal, and language preferences before generating new tracks.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Simple Playlist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear mood.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-song playlist for a calm rainy evening. Use my available music history if I approve it.

prompt_templates.title: Use Manual Taste Input
prompt_templates.scenario: User does not want local app scanning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a playlist from this taste description instead of scanning files: I like mellow R&B, city pop, soft female vocals, and warm piano textures.

prompt_templates.title: Balance Genres And Languages
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a six-song commute playlist with upbeat energy, mostly J-pop and English indie pop, plus one instrumental track.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Full Playlist Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using generation, artwork, playback, and feedback.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my approved listening sources, create a late-night playlist, generate matching songs and cover art, play the result, and ask for feedback afterward.

output_examples.input: Create a late night chill playlist.
output: A concise taste profile with source counts, top genres, moods, vocal balance, and top artists.
A five-song playlist plan with genre, mood, vocal or instrumental tag, and short song descriptions.
Generated audio file paths, cover art location, and a feedback prompt after playback.

output_examples.input: Use my Spotify export to make a workout playlist.
output: A request to confirm the export file before use.
A filtered profile based on meaningful listening events, excluding short skips and sensitive fields.
An energetic playlist plan with generated tracks saved under the playlist folder.
A list of available generated playlist names.
Playback of the selected playlist in filename order when a supported player is installed.
File paths shown instead when no local audio player is available.

best_practices: Ask for consent before scanning music apps, searching files, or using exported listening history.
Review where profile, cache, playlist, and feedback files will be stored before generation.
Provide a clear theme, song count, and language or genre constraints for better results.
anti_patterns: Do not scan broad local folders without user approval.
Do not include Spotify sensitive fields such as IP address in profiles or saved files.
Do not run paid or quota-limited generation commands without confirming the user wants generation.

faq.question: Does this skill need access to my music history?
faq.answer: It works best with approved Apple Music data, a Spotify export, or a manual taste description. You can choose manual input only.

faq.question: Does it upload my listening history?
faq.answer: The skill instructs local profiling, but it may query MusicBrainz for artist metadata and use mmx for generation prompts. Review data use before running it.

faq.question: Can it use Spotify directly?
faq.answer: No. It asks for a Spotify data export because useful Spotify listening history is not stored locally.

faq.question: What does it generate?
faq.answer: It can generate songs, a square cover image, playlist metadata, and a saved feedback record when the required tools are available.

faq.question: Which tools are required?
faq.answer: The full workflow requires mmx CLI authentication, Python 3, and a local audio player such as mpv, ffplay, or afplay.

faq.question: Can it work in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. User-facing text follows the user prompt language, while internal mmx generation prompts are written in English for quality.
